abilities.description: Creates an explosively charged image of Storm Spirit that lasts 12 seconds and will detonate and deal damage if an enemy unit comes near it.
abilities.name: Static Remnant
abilities.story: Raijin Thunderkeg's duality allowed him to admire himself in shocking fashion.

abilities.description: A vortex that pulls an enemy unit to Storm Spirit's location.
abilities.name: Electric Vortex
abilities.story: Raijin's thunderous, boisterous energy often draws others into an electrifying situation.

abilities.description: Casting a spell creates an electrical charge, which is released in a burst on Storm's next attack, dealing damage and slowing nearby enemies.

abilities.name: Overload

abilities.description: Storm Spirit becomes volatile electricity, charging across the battlefield until he depletes his mana or reaches his target. The activation mana cost is 25+7.5% of his total mana pool, and the cost per 100 units traveled is 10+0.65% of his total mana pool. Damage is expressed in damage per 100 units traveled.
params: Passive Component[picture:talent_tree_symbol] Auto Static Remnant Drop Distance: 500
abilities.name: Ball Lightning

description: Zips across the map to engage in fights. In a final act of desperation, the elementalist Thunderkeg cast a suicidal spell that merged his being with the Storm Celestial. From this fusion, the Storm Spirit was born. Now known by his combined name, Raijin Thunderkeg leaves behind crackling Static Remnant of himself, boastful poses that shock foes as he pulls them into an Electric Vortex. Every ability he casts Overloads him with energy, which he gleefully discharges on the next unfortunate target. Like a jolly weatherman, Raijin forecasts victory with a boisterous laugh. The Storm Spirit flashes across the land in the form of Ball of Lightning, zapping his enemies senseless, and zipping away before any can hope to catch him. trivia: Storm Spirit, like Brewmaster, Earth Spirit and Ember Spirit, was a Pandaren in DotA. He was re-skinned to avoid copyright issues with "Blizzard Entertainment", since Pandaren is a race in the "Warcraft" Universe.
